summary: easyaillm2 setup.py fetches and executes Pastebin payload at install time
details: On `pip install easyaillm2`, setup.py fetches a raw text body from https://pastebin.com/raw/yBcUM1QB and passes the first line directly to `os.system('cmd /c "..."')`, executing whatever the mutable, anonymous Pastebin paste currently serves with the installer's privileges. There is no integrity check, no version pinning, and no relationship between the destination and any legitimate publisher. The package itself ships no module code (the source tree contains only egg-info), and its name/description mimic LLM-tooling naming (`easyaillm2` / `easyllama2`) — the install-time Pastebin dropper is the package's only behavior. A Pastebin owner can swap the payload at any moment, turning every future `pip install` of this version into arbitrary remote code execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
